details.body: Add a test for `br ready` failure (e.g., br binary not found or timeout during the ready query). The code at lines 505-518 of `src/cli/run.rs` correctly transitions to `NeedsOperator` when `request_ready_milestone_beads` returns `Err`, but no test exercises this path. All four existing tests cover bv failure or successful br queries, leaving the br-failure → NeedsOperator branch untested.

Remove the unnecessary `async` qualifier from `transition_controller_for_selection_outcome` (line 448 of `src/cli/run.rs`). The function contains no `.await` calls — it only calls the synchronous `sync_controller_state`. Making it `async` forces every caller to `.await` a future that resolves immediately, adding unnecessary overhead.

Wire the new selector into a real controller path before approving this bead. `select_next_milestone_bead` is implemented at `src/cli/run.rs:470`, but there are no production call sites; the only references are the new unit tests at `src/cli/run.rs:1873`, `src/cli/run.rs:1921`, `src/cli/run.rs:1961`, and `src/cli/run.rs:1994`. At the same time, successful reconciliation still moves the controller back to `Selecting` at `src/cli/run.rs:1030`, while the start/resume paths continue to only prepare already bead-linked projects at `src/cli/run.rs:1244` and `src/cli/run.rs:1363`. As shipped, the controller never actually asks `bv` for the next bead in production and will stall in `selecting` after a completed bead.

Tighten `br_show_error_is_missing` so only real 'bead not found' exits are treated as missing. The current check at `src/cli/run.rs:313` treats any `BrExitError` containing `unknown` in stdout/stderr as a missing bead, and `request_bead_detail` then converts that into `Ok(None)` at `src/cli/run.rs:366`. That will misclassify real tool failures such as `unknown flag` or `unknown subcommand` as a blocked/missing bead instead of sending the controller to `needs_operator`, which violates the selection policy for `br` failures.

Add a test for the code path at run.rs:540-555 where bv recommends a bead that is not part of the milestone plan. This path transitions to Blocked with a reason containing 'not part of milestone', but no test exercises it. A test should create a milestone with known beads, have bv recommend a bead ID outside that set, and assert the controller lands in Blocked with the expected reason message.

In the NoRecommendation path (run.rs:588-613), when bv reports no actionable bead but br shows ready milestone beads, the controller goes to Blocked. Consider whether this should be NeedsOperator instead, since the discrepancy suggests bv may be misconfigured or out of sync. If the current Blocked behavior is intentional, add a code comment explaining why Blocked is preferred over NeedsOperator for this case, and add a test covering this specific sub-path (bv returns no recommendation, br returns non-empty ready list).

Wire the new selector into the automation-runtime reconciliation path, not just the CLI post-run helpers. `reconcile_success` still returns non-final milestones to `Selecting` at `src/contexts/automation_runtime/success_reconciliation.rs:384-400`, and the daemon's success path only logs the hint afterward at `src/contexts/automation_runtime/daemon_loop.rs:1945-1975`. The new selector is invoked only from the CLI wrappers in `src/cli/run.rs:1405-1418`, `src/cli/run.rs:1542-1555`, and `src/cli/run.rs:1585-1596`, so daemon-driven milestone execution will still stall in `selecting` instead of performing the bv/br validation flow.

Persist the bv recommendation before the `br ready` validation step. In `src/cli/run.rs:492-517`, a successful `bv --robot-next` result is kept only in memory; if `br ready` then times out or fails, the controller moves to `needs_operator` with a reason that no longer mentions which bead bv recommended. That violates the stated selection policy to record the recommendation before claiming and leaves operators without the key audit datum for the failed selection attempt.

In `src/cli/run.rs:persist_next_step_recommendation` (line 381-383), when `std::fs::rename` fails the `.tmp` file is left behind. The counterpart in `success_reconciliation.rs:persist_next_step_hint` (line 530) cleans up with `let _ = std::fs::remove_file(&tmp_path);`. Add the same cleanup to the `run.rs` version for consistency and to avoid accumulating orphaned temp files.

In `success_reconciliation.rs` (lines 227-241), `select_next_milestone_bead` failures are mapped to `ReconciliationError::MilestoneUpdateFailed` which the daemon (`daemon_loop.rs:1983-2007`) treats as a task failure requiring operator intervention. But by this point the bead is already closed and synced (steps 1-2 completed). A selection failure for the *next* bead should not retroactively fail the *current* bead's reconciliation outcome. Consider wrapping the `select_next_milestone_bead` call in a best-effort handler (log + continue) similar to hint capture, or introduce a distinct error variant so the daemon can complete the current task while flagging the selection failure separately.

Allow `run sync-milestone` / terminal-sync replays to re-enter selection from a blocked controller. Right now `controller_requires_terminal_task_sync` hard-returns `false` for `Blocked` in `src/cli/run.rs:690-694`, and `continue_selecting_milestone_bead_if_needed` only calls the selector when the controller is already `Selecting` in `src/cli/run.rs:731-736`. Combined with the `exact_attempt_already_terminal` gate in `src/cli/run.rs:1145-1156`, a completed bead-backed project that previously landed in `Blocked` can never be re-evaluated by rerunning `run sync-milestone`, even though the controller model explicitly supports `Blocked -> Selecting` when ready work appears in `src/contexts/milestone_record/controller.rs:965-975`. Because completed projects cannot use `run resume`, this makes blocked selection a permanent dead-end. Make terminal sync invoke the blocked-state resume path (or otherwise retry selection from `Blocked`) and add a regression test covering a completed project that becomes ready after initially blocking.

Stop querying `bv --robot-next` twice during daemon reconciliation. `reconcile_success` already captures and persists a `NextBeadResponse` in `src/contexts/automation_runtime/success_reconciliation.rs:197-205`, but for non-final milestones it then calls `select_next_milestone_bead` in `src/contexts/automation_runtime/success_reconciliation.rs:227-235`, which performs a second `bv --robot-next` call in `src/cli/run.rs:329-347` and validates/claims based on that second response in `src/cli/run.rs:538-556`. The daemon still reports the first hint in `src/contexts/automation_runtime/daemon_loop.rs:1970-1974`, so the logged/persisted recommendation can differ from the bead actually claimed, and a transient failure on the second bv call can move the controller to `needs_operator` even though the first call already produced a valid recommendation. Thread the first recommendation into the selection validator (or refactor selection to accept a pre-fetched recommendation) so the recommendation that gets recorded is exactly the one that is validated and acted on.

`src/contexts/automation_runtime/success_reconciliation.rs:229-263` swallows any error from the new next-bead selection step after a non-final reconciliation, and `src/contexts/automation_runtime/success_reconciliation.rs:428-445` has already moved the controller back to `selecting` by that point. A corrupted/missing `plan.json`, controller write failure, or other `select_next_milestone_bead*()` setup error therefore leaves the milestone stuck in `selecting` with only a transient log/stdout warning. That undermines the new safe-state contract: this path should persist a `needs_operator` or `blocked` transition with the failure reason instead of leaving an apparently healthy selecting controller.

In `select_next_milestone_bead_from_recommendation` (src/cli/run.rs:676-679), the controller transition reason says 'requesting the next bead recommendation from bv before any claim' even though this function receives a pre-fetched recommendation and never calls bv. The `Selecting` state reason should reflect reality, e.g. 'validating a pre-fetched bv recommendation against br readiness before any claim'. This matters because operators inspect controller state reasons for debugging.

In `persist_next_step_recommendation` (src/cli/run.rs:354-385), all error paths use `eprintln!` for logging. In a daemon context, stderr may not be monitored. Consider using `tracing::warn!` (already used elsewhere in the codebase, e.g. success_reconciliation.rs:267) for consistency, so these failures appear in structured logs alongside the tracing infrastructure the daemon already uses.

Fix the post-reconciliation/post-sync selecting audit trail. `select_next_milestone_bead_from_recommendation` and `select_next_milestone_bead` both call `sync_controller_state(... Selecting ...)` in `src/cli/run.rs:694-703` and `src/cli/run.rs:730-739`, but the real call sites already moved the controller to `Selecting` in `src/contexts/automation_runtime/success_reconciliation.rs:188-199,239-253` and `src/cli/run.rs:1310-1330`. Because `sync_controller_state` checkpoints same-state requests instead of recording the new reason (`src/contexts/milestone_record/controller.rs:620-623`), the journal never captures the new “requesting the next bead recommendation…” / “validating a pre-fetched…” reasons on the production paths. Either transition through a distinct state or add a same-state path that updates the reason/journal entry so operators can see the actual selection step.

Normalize bead IDs in restart-time ready checks. The new selection code added qualified/unqualified matching helpers in `src/cli/run.rs:272-296`, but `ProjectMilestoneControllerRuntime::has_ready_beads` still does a raw `planned_refs.contains(bead.id)` at `src/cli/run.rs:938-944`. `resume_controller` now relies on that method to keep `Selecting` controllers active and to unblock `Blocked` controllers (`src/contexts/milestone_record/controller.rs:815-824` and `src/contexts/milestone_record/controller.rs:967-975`). If `br ready` and the milestone plan use different bead-id forms, a restart will incorrectly report “no ready beads” even though the fresh selection path would find one. Reuse `milestone_planned_refs_contain`/canonicalization here and add a restart test for the mixed-ID case.

`src/cli/run.rs:1310-1330` moves a completed milestone back to `selecting`, but the follow-up selection path in `src/cli/run.rs:728-763` and `src/cli/run.rs:786-855` just propagates any `AppError` from `select_next_milestone_bead` instead of persisting a safe fallback state. If plan loading, controller journal writes, or other local persistence fails during selection, `run start`, `run resume`, or `run sync-milestone` exits with an error and leaves the controller durably stuck in `selecting` with no operator-visible reason. That violates the bead requirement that mismatches/tool failures land in `blocked` or `needs_operator`. Add the same `needs_operator` fallback used in `persist_selection_failure_after_reconciliation` for this CLI sync path.

Preserve non-final reconciliation replay idempotency after automatic next-bead selection. `reconcile_success` still begins every replay by forcing the old bead/task back through `sync_controller_task_reconciling` (`src/contexts/automation_runtime/success_reconciliation.rs:159-171`), but the new post-reconciliation selection path can already have advanced the controller to the next bead (`src/contexts/automation_runtime/success_reconciliation.rs:239-253`). Once that happens, `sync_controller_task_reconciling` rejects the replay because `validate_active_context_alignment` treats the controller's new active bead/task as a hard mismatch (`src/contexts/milestone_record/controller.rs:471-489`, `src/contexts/milestone_record/controller.rs:1339-1348`). That regresses the reconciler's crash-safe replay behavior for any non-final milestone whose first reconciliation successfully selected the next bead. Add the same kind of exact-attempt/current-context guard the CLI sync path uses, and cover a replay-after-nonfinal-selection test.

Normalize the bead ID that gets persisted into the controller when selection succeeds. The new selector intentionally supports mixed qualified/unqualified IDs via `canonicalize_milestone_bead_ref`, `milestone_planned_refs_contain`, and `milestone_bead_refs_match` (`src/cli/run.rs:272-296`, `src/cli/run.rs:584-585`), but on success it still writes the raw `br ready` string into `active_bead_id` (`src/cli/run.rs:588-594`). Downstream controller code still compares bead IDs with plain string equality when adopting a project or deciding whether a terminal sync applies (`src/contexts/milestone_record/controller.rs:1339-1348`, `src/cli/run.rs:780-781`, `src/cli/run.rs:1016-1021`). If `br ready` reports `bead-2` and the bead-linked project uses `ms-alpha.bead-2`, the controller will later reject the real project as a mismatched bead or skip its terminal sync. Canonicalize the stored claimed bead ID, or canonicalize all downstream comparisons, and add a regression that carries a mixed-form selected bead through claim/start/sync.
